items.summary: Add a backend room system: users can create a named room (generating a unique room code) or join an existing room by entering a room code. The backend must store room state including a list of active participants and all drawing strokes (as serialized canvas data or stroke arrays) so late joiners can see the current canvas state.

Implement real-time collaborative drawing: all participants in a room share a single canvas. When one user draws a stroke, it is persisted to the backend and all other participants polling the room receive the new strokes and see them rendered on their canvas.

Implement in-room voice messaging: users can record a short voice message, which is encoded as base64 audio (WebM/Opus or similar) and stored in the backend against the room. All participants in the room can see and play back received voice messages in a message list panel.

Build a Room UI: replace the existing sender/recipient pairing flow with a unified room-based lobby. The home page should offer 'Create Room' and 'Join Room' options. After entering/creating a room, users land on a room page that shows: the shared drawing canvas (with color and stroke controls), a voice message panel with record button and message history, a participant count or list, and the room code for sharing.
Update the TanStack Router routes to include a /room/:code route for the shared room page, and update App.tsx accordingly. Preserve existing / route for the updated home/lobby page.
